scenarios.starting_prompt: Hi, I need to book a flight.
scenarios.conversation_plan: Ask the agent to find economy cash flights from SFO to NRT on 2026-07-23. Confirm the results were returned and note how many flights were listed.

scenarios.starting_prompt: Find me all economy flights from JFK to CDG on 2026-06-07.
scenarios.conversation_plan: Request the full flight list from JFK to CDG on 2026-06-07 in economy class. Once results arrive, ask the agent which flight has the shortest duration.

scenarios.starting_prompt: I want to fly to Tokyo next month.
scenarios.conversation_plan: Start vague — no origin, no date. When the agent asks for missing details, provide: origin SFO, date 2026-07-23, economy class. Confirm the results were returned.

scenarios.starting_prompt: Show me cash flights from LAX to NRT.
scenarios.conversation_plan: Ask for cash flights LAX to NRT. When asked for a date, say 2026-06-07 in economy. After results arrive, ask how many non-stop options are available.

user_persona.description: A busy professional who wants quick, direct answers with no fluff.

scenarios.starting_prompt: What economy flights are available from SFO to NRT on July 23rd 2026?
scenarios.conversation_plan: Ask for the flight list. After receiving results, ask the agent to show only flights with fewer than 2 stops. If the agent cannot filter, note it and end the conversation.

scenarios.starting_prompt: Find economy flights from SFO to NRT on 2026-07-23.
scenarios.conversation_plan: Step 1: Ask for economy flights SFO to NRT on 2026-07-23 and wait for the full list. Step 2: Ask 'Show me ANA only' and observe whether the agent filters or explains it cannot. Step 3: Ask 'How about all nonstop options instead?' and see if the agent can highlight nonstop flights from the already-returned list or re-explains scope. Step 4: Ask 'Show me flights under 12 hours total' and observe the response. At each step, note whether the agent provides a helpful, accurate reply or gracefully declines with an explanation. End the conversation after step 4.

scenarios.starting_prompt: I'm looking for economy flights from LAX to NRT on June 7th 2026.
scenarios.conversation_plan: Step 1: Request the full flight list for LAX to NRT on 2026-06-07 economy. Step 2: After results arrive, ask 'Can you show me only flights under 10 hours?' and note how the agent responds. Step 3: Follow up with 'What about under 8 hours?' — check if the agent responds consistently. Step 4: Ask 'Which of these is the shortest flight?' to see if the agent can answer a factual question about the results without filtering. Evaluate whether the agent maintains context across all four turns and gives coherent, accurate answers.

scenarios.starting_prompt: I have flexible dates — can you find me economy flights from JFK to CDG sometime between July 1st and July 7th 2026?
scenarios.conversation_plan: Step 1: Ask for economy flights JFK to CDG across the date range 2026-07-01 to 2026-07-07 (7 days, ~120 flights total). Wait for the agent to return results. Step 2: Ask 'How many total flights did you find across all dates?' — verify the agent reports an accurate count without hallucinating or under-counting. Step 3: Ask 'Which day has the most flight options?' — check whether the agent correctly identifies the date with the highest flight count from the actual data. Step 4: Ask 'What is the cheapest flight available across all those dates?' — verify the agent identifies the correct minimum-price flight without fabricating prices. Evaluate accuracy of all factual claims against the returned data.

scenarios.starting_prompt: What economy flights are there from JFK to CDG (Paris) between July 1st and July 7th 2026?
scenarios.conversation_plan: Step 1: Ask for the full range search JFK to CDG 2026-07-01 to 2026-07-07. After results arrive, ask 'Which day is cheapest to fly?' Step 2: Ask 'Are there any nonstop options? If so, which dates have them?' — verify the agent does not fabricate nonstop routes that don't exist. Step 3: Ask 'Show me only flights under $400' — observe whether the agent attempts to filter or gracefully explains it cannot. Step 4: Ask 'What about business class on July 4th?' — this requires a new search with different cabin/date params; evaluate whether the agent correctly triggers a new search or explains the limitation. End after step 4.

user_persona.description: A price-conscious traveler who scrutinizes every fare and pushes hard for the cheapest option.

behaviors.name: Price-focused probing
behaviors.description: Always asks about the cheapest option and pushes back if the answer seems too expensive.
behavior_instructions: Always ask which option is cheapest.
If a price seems high, ask 'Is that really the best price?' or 'Any cheaper options?'
Express mild skepticism about prices: 'That seems expensive.'
violation_rubrics: Agent states a price without specifying the flight it belongs to.
Agent claims a price is 'the cheapest' without referencing the actual data.
Agent fabricates a price not present in the returned flight list.
